Here is another account from apostate times in Israel: "In those days there was no king in Israel, but every man did that which was right in his own eyes" (Judg. 17:6 and fn. a-c). It was no doubt the lack of informed and conscientious priesthood leaders rather than lack of a king that caused Israel to flounder. A young Levite from Bethlehem of Judah went to Ephraim, where he was employed by…
